title: Indorouchera contestiana
intro: Indorouchera contestiana, also known as Rouchera, is a plant species native to tropical Africa, belonging to the family Moraceae. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory infections, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Indorouchera contestiana include flavonoids, terpenoids, and alkaloids, which have been reported to possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. The most common herbal preparations made from Indorouchera contestiana include decoctions, infusions, and ointments, which are used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory infections, skin conditions, and wounds.
